allowed_tones.description: Energetic, forward-looking, progress-focused. Use for streak reinforcement and goal achievement.
Warm, understanding, non-judgmental. Use for users who have missed sessions or are struggling.
Career and life-goal oriented, dream-focused. Use for working professionals and students.
Casual: Conversational, peer-like, informal Hindi-English mix. Use for Tier 2/3 users, daily nudges.
Gentle: Creates mild time pressure without anxiety. Use for trial expiry and streak-at-risk scenarios.
Praise-heavy, milestone-focused. Use for achievements, streaks, coin milestones.

disallowed_tones.description: Tripping: Never make users feel bad about missing sessions. Avoid "you failed", "you broke your streak again".
Avoid stiff English that feels distant. This is a consumer app for everyday Indians.
Mongering: Do not use anxiety-inducing language around job loss, social rejection, or failure.
Avoid generic "Great job!" without specific context.
No "BUY NOW", "LIMITED TIME ONLY" pressure tactics.

example_hooks: "Join 1M+ Indians transforming their careers with English"
"Be part of the English revolution in Tier 2 India"
"You completed 5 lessons! Keep the momentum going"
"Your pronunciation score improved by 12% this week"
"Practice the scenario that matters most to you"
"Your 50 coins are waiting to be spent"
"Your Sia conversation history is building your profile"
"3 friends joined this week. Invite more!"
"Rahul from Lucknow is 2 lessons ahead of you"
"Only 2 days left in your free trial"
"Open the app to see your mystery challenge"
"Don't lose your leaderboard rank — practice now"

disallowed: Hooks that reference competitor apps or compare negatively
Hooks that imply the user is "falling behind in life" (shame-based)
Hooks that make unrealistic promises ("Speak fluent English in 7 days")
Hooks exploiting financial insecurity ("Lose your job if you don't improve")
